articles.title: Google Photos launches an AI try-on feature for clothes you already have
articles.summary: Google Photos launches an AI-powered virtual try-on feature that organizes clothes from your photos into a digital wardrobe, allowing you to mix and match outfits and share looks with friends.

articles.title: Live updates from Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s court battle over the future of OpenAI
articles.summary: Elon Musk sues Sam Altman and OpenAI, claiming the company abandoned its nonprofit mission to benefit humanity in favor of profit maximization. The high-stakes trial began April 27th with jury selection and could reshape OpenAI's future.

articles.title: Tumbler Ridge families are suing OpenAI
articles.summary: Seven families of Tumbler Ridge school shooting victims are suing OpenAI and CEO Sam Altman, claiming the company failed to alert police about the suspect's suspicious ChatGPT activity to protect its reputation and IPO.

articles.title: Google Photos uses AI to make the iconic closet from ‘Clueless’ a reality
articles.summary: Google uses AI to recreate Cher's iconic closet from the movie 'Clueless,' bringing the fictional wardrobe to life with artificial intelligence technology.

articles.title: More Gemini features are coming to Google TV
articles.summary: Google TV gains new Gemini features including photo and video transformation tools Nano Banana and Veo, enhancing creative capabilities.

articles.title: Firestorm Labs raises $82M to take drone factories into the field
articles.summary: Firestorm Labs raised $82M to deploy mobile drone factories in shipping containers for frontline manufacturing. The innovation enables rapid drone production directly in combat zones.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a cloud platform with 2 million developers, raised $100 million Series B funding to build AI-native infrastructure challenging AWS and Google Cloud amid growing demand for AI applications.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code's $20-$200 monthly pricing has prompted developers to seek alternatives. Goose, a free open-source AI coding agent by Block, offers similar functionality without subscription fees or cloud dependency.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B after a viral billboard campaign featuring AI tokens and coding challenges to recruit engineers. The creative hiring stunt decoded to a puzzle about Berlin nightclub selection algorithms.

articles.title: Why a recent supply-chain attack singled out security firms Checkmarx and Bitwarden
articles.summary: Security firm Checkmarx suffered multiple supply-chain attacks in six weeks, including compromised GitHub accounts delivering malware to customers. The attacks also targeted password manager Bitwarden and exploited the Trivy vulnerability scanner.

articles.title: Open source package with 1 million monthly downloads stole user credentials
articles.summary: A popular open-source package with 1 million monthly downloads was compromised, allowing attackers to steal user credentials including API tokens and cloud keys through a malicious version.

articles.title: Why are top university websites serving porn? It comes down to shoddy housekeeping.
articles.summary: Prestigious university websites like Berkeley, Columbia, and Washington University are serving pornography and malware through exploited subdomains due to poor administrative record-keeping. Hundreds of subdomains across 34+ universities have been compromised.

articles.title: The Download: storing nuclear waste and orchestrating agents
articles.summary: Nuclear energy gains political support and tech industry investment, but the US urgently needs a permanent solution for the 2,000 metric tons of high-level waste produced annually by reactors.

articles.title: It’s time to make a plan for nuclear waste
articles.summary: Nuclear energy is experiencing renewed interest from tech companies, but the US lacks a long-term solution for managing 2,000 metric tons of high-level waste produced annually by reactors.

articles.title: The Download: Musk and Altman’s legal showdown, and AI’s profit problem
articles.summary: Elon Musk sues OpenAI's Sam Altman, claiming deception and seeking $134 billion in damages plus conversion back to non-profit status. The trial could impact OpenAI's planned IPO and leadership.

articles.title: AI evals are becoming the new compute bottleneck
articles.summary: AI evaluation has become a major bottleneck in AI development, consuming significant computational resources. Organizations struggle with the cost and complexity of assessing model performance at scale.

articles.title: Granite 4.1 LLMs: How They’re Built
articles.summary: Granite 4.1 LLMs represent advanced language models built with improved architectures and training methods. These models demonstrate enhanced performance across various tasks through optimized design and processing techniques.

articles.title: DeepInfra on Hugging Face Inference Providers 🔥
articles.summary: DeepInfra has been added to Hugging Face's inference providers, expanding deployment options for machine learning models with faster inference capabilities.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses future directions in AI development. The conversation explores emerging trends and next steps in artificial intelligence technology.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels are leveraging Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and boost creative capabilities in content handling and marketing initiatives.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft has open sourced its 'farm of the future' toolkit, making agricultural AI technology publicly available for farmers and developers to improve farming practices and sustainability.

articles.title: Celebrating 20 years of Google Translate: Fun facts, tips and new features to try
articles.summary: Google Translate celebrates 20 years of translation service, evolving from a 2006 AI experiment to support nearly 250 languages today. The company shares fun facts and new features marking the milestone.

articles.title: Join the new AI Agents Vibe Coding Course from Google and Kaggle
articles.summary: Google and Kaggle are offering a 5-Day AI Agents Intensive Course with open registration. The program teaches practical AI agent development skills through hands-on coding.

articles.title: 8 Gemini tips for organizing your space (and life)
articles.summary: Google Gemini offers AI-powered tips for organizing both physical homes and digital spaces, including cleaning schedules, inbox decluttering, and seasonal chore management.

articles.title: Announcing our partnership with the Republic of Korea
articles.summary: Google DeepMind partners with the Republic of Korea to accelerate scientific breakthroughs using frontier AI models.

articles.title: Decoupled DiLoCo: A new frontier for resilient, distributed AI training
articles.summary: Decoupled DiLoCo introduces a new method for resilient distributed AI training that improves fault tolerance and efficiency. The approach enables more reliable large-scale model training across multiple systems.

articles.title: Partnering with industry leaders to accelerate AI transformation
articles.summary: Google DeepMind partners with global consultancies to help organizations worldwide leverage frontier AI technology for business transformation.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows, featuring higher per-token prices but improved token efficiency. Mistral open-sourced the Small 4 model family while the podcast discussed major AI developments including Mamba 3 and attention residuals.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia launches DLSS 5, a generative AI technology that enhances real-time video game graphics with photorealism. The advancement signals AI's expanding role beyond gaming applications.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: Episode 237 discusses major AI developments including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub PR review pricing, and new interactive visualization features from ChatGPT and Anthropic.

articles.title: IDC: How EMEA CIOs can jumpstart AI rollouts
articles.summary: EMEA CIOs must audit systems to accelerate stalled AI rollouts. Only 9% of organizations achieved measurable business outcomes, with most projects hampered by execution issues and demands for financial validation.

articles.title: GPT-5.5 is OpenAI’s most capable agentic AI model yet
articles.summary: OpenAI launched GPT-5.5, its most capable agentic AI model, designed to plan, use tools, and work independently on tasks. It reduces the need for multiple prompts and human course-correction.

articles.title: IBM launches AI platform Bob to regulate SDLC costs
articles.summary: IBM launches Bob, an AI platform designed to manage software development lifecycle costs and governance while maintaining development speed. It integrates governance controls and security standards throughout the entire development process.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to identify which agents cause task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, addressing debugging challenges in collaborative AI systems.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduced Astra, a dual-model architecture for autonomous robot navigation that addresses key challenges in complex indoor environments by answering where robots are, where they're going, and how to get there.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights using reinforcement learning.

articles.title: Claude Code is leaking API keys into public package registries
articles.summary: A study reveals that Claude Code and similar AI coding assistants are inadvertently leaking sensitive API keys to public code repositories and package registries.

articles.title: Anthropic’s MCP vulnerability: When ‘expected behavior’ becomes a supply chain nightmare
articles.summary: Security researchers discovered a critical architectural flaw in Anthropic's Model Context Protocol that exposes millions of AI applications to remote takeovers, creating a significant supply chain security threat.

articles.title: The paradox of LLM self-distillation: Faster reasoning, weaker generalization
articles.summary: Self-distilling LLMs for faster reasoning sacrifices their ability to explore alternative solutions on difficult problems, revealing a hidden trade-off between speed and generalization capability.

articles.title: HP vs. Dell: I've tested dozens of laptops from both brands, and here's my advice
articles.summary: Dell and HP both produce excellent laptops with distinct strengths. The article compares their models to help buyers choose based on their specific needs.

articles.title: Our readers can't stop buying these 10 gadgets - and No. 4 really surprised us
articles.summary: ZDNET readers are consistently purchasing ten specific gadgets throughout the year, with the fourth item on the list proving unexpectedly popular among the publication's audience.

articles.title: Is Facebook adding Gen Z phrases to your shared posts? You're not alone, bestie
articles.summary: Facebook users have reported unexpected Gen Z slang appearing in their posts over the past two weeks, likely due to AI. The platform may be automatically adding trendy phrases to user content.

articles.title: Building AI Agents in Python with Pydantic AI
articles.summary: Pydantic AI enables developers to build AI agents in Python efficiently. The framework simplifies agent creation with structured data validation and seamless LLM integration.

articles.title: Effective Context Engineering for AI Agents: A Developer’s Guide
articles.summary: A guide for developers on effective context engineering for AI agents, helping them optimize how information is provided to AI systems for better performance and accuracy.

articles.title: Text Summarization with Scikit-LLM
articles.summary: Scikit-LLM enables text summarization using machine learning techniques. This tool integrates with scikit-learn for efficient document summarization tasks.

articles.title: 4 YAML Files Instead of PySpark: How We Let Analysts Build Data Pipelines Without Engineers
articles.summary: A company replaced Python-based PySpark pipelines with dlt, dbt, and Trino using YAML configuration, enabling analysts to build data pipelines independently and reducing delivery time from weeks to one day.

articles.title: Ensembles of Ensembles of Ensembles: A Guide to Stacking
articles.summary: The article discusses stacking, a machine learning technique combining multiple models for improved performance. It argues that ensemble methods outperform single models.

articles.title: Agentic AI: How to Save on Tokens
articles.summary: Article discusses techniques to reduce token usage in agentic AI systems, including caching, lazy-loading, routing, and compaction strategies.

articles.title: NVIDIA Launches Nemotron 3 Nano Omni Model, Unifying Vision, Audio and Language for up to 9x More Efficient AI Agents
articles.summary: NVIDIA launched Nemotron 3 Nano Omni, a unified multimodal AI model combining vision, audio, and language capabilities into one system, delivering up to 9x more efficient AI agents with faster responses and advanced reasoning.

articles.title: Into the Omniverse: Manufacturing’s Simulation-First Era Has Arrived
articles.summary: Manufacturing is entering a simulation-first era using NVIDIA Omniverse and OpenUSD technology, replacing traditional design-build-test cycles with reliable virtual testing environments.

articles.title: OpenAI’s New GPT-5.5 Powers Codex on NVIDIA Infrastructure — and NVIDIA Is Already Putting It to Work
articles.summary: OpenAI's GPT-5.5 powers Codex, an agentic coding application running on NVIDIA's GB200 infrastructure. Over 10,000 NVIDIA employees across departments report significant productivity gains using the new system.

articles.title: Building AI-ready data: Vanguard’s Virtual Analyst journey
articles.summary: Vanguard developed a Virtual Analyst using conversational AI to help financial analysts query complex datasets without writing SQL queries. The project demonstrated that successful AI deployment requires building robust, AI-ready data infrastructure beyond selecting foundation models.

articles.title: Run custom MCP proxies serverless on Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Runtime
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Runtime enables deploying serverless MCP proxies for AI agents, providing governance, security controls, and observability for tool integrations through the Model Context Protocol.

articles.title: Migrating a text agent to a voice assistant with Amazon Nova 2 Sonic
articles.summary: Amazon Nova 2 Sonic enables migrating text agents to voice assistants for faster, more natural interactions. The solution addresses architecture requirements, design priorities, and tool integration across finance, healthcare, and retail industries.

articles.title: Powering AI Factories with NVIDIA Enterprise Reference Architectures
articles.summary: NVIDIA launches Enterprise Reference Architectures to power AI factories, enabling organizations to deploy agentic AI systems for reasoning, automation, and real-time decision-making at scale.

articles.title: Scaling Biomolecular Modeling Using Context Parallelism in NVIDIA BioNeMo
articles.summary: NVIDIA BioNeMo uses context parallelism to overcome GPU memory limitations in biomolecular modeling, enabling researchers to study complex biological systems without fragmenting them into isolated components.

articles.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Nano Omni Powers Multimodal Agent Reasoning in a Single Efficient Open Model
articles.summary: NVIDIA's Nemotron 3 Nano Omni is an efficient open model enabling multimodal agent reasoning across vision, audio, and text in a single integrated system, replacing fragmented model chains.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: Research study examines generational differences in consumer attitudes toward social media ads across platforms, exploring how age groups perceive advertising differently.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta commits to achieving net zero emissions by 2030, developing technology to reduce its carbon footprint and making solutions publicly available.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with a non-traditional background developed cutting-edge extended reality technology, demonstrating how unconventional paths can lead to innovation in emerging tech fields.

articles.title: Gradient-based Planning for World Models at Longer Horizons
articles.summary: GRASP is a gradient-based planner for world models enabling long-horizon planning through trajectory lifting, stochastic exploration, and gradient reshaping to improve optimization in high-dimensional visual spaces.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers develop methods to identify interactions at scale in Large Language Models to improve interpretability and transparency. This advances understanding of LLM decision-making through feature attribution analysis for safer, more trustworthy AI.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research on information-driven design of imaging systems that uses encoders to map objects to images while accounting for noise corruption. An information estimator quantifies measurement quality using only noisy data and noise models.
